title: pandorea pandorana subsp. pandorana
intro: Pandorea pandorana subsp. pandorana, also known as the Australian passionflower or Wonga vine, is a plant species native to eastern Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. It has been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, colds, and respiratory problems, as well as skin conditions and wounds, due to its health benefits in promoting wound healing and reducing inflammation. In herbal preparations, Pandorea pandorana subsp. pandorana is often used as a tincture, tea, or poultice to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
